text:
Okay, first and foremost the power button is 8n the STUPIDEST place possible. Directly in the center of the flat side, right where you're going to hit it every time you change your grip, not so much shaving your face but trying to shave my head I must have turned the razor off a dozen times.
The foil heads are set up one above the other by about a quarter of an inch. You want both foils contacting your skin at the same time so you need to use an angle. It works but it's awkward.
I got a decent shave with a couple patchy spots but I had to go over my head at least twenty times.
Cleaning is easy, either blow the beard dust out/off of the foils, the cutters and the cover or rinse it under the faucet real quick. I recommend dry cleaning until you're sure your done, then doing a thorough job under the faucet.
The case is handy though I had to squeeze the USB-C cable in next to the unit. The net area for storage puts the cable on top of the unit and you can't zipper it closed. I've always got a wall wart and nine foot cable when I travel so not having one in the razor case isn't a problem. The case is handy to keep everything else you own clean.
Unit isn't excessively loud. Though there's cracking noises from the posts the cutters are mounted on and the foil can flex and dent in.
Not my favorite razor but not the worst I've used.

This is a not so cheaply priced but definitely cheap knock off of the real deal.. I am an avid Amazon shopper (just ask my husband). I am not being paid by anyone for this review. I'm just trying to warn other women (and men) who use the product not to waste their money. PLEASE LOOK AT THE PICTURES I POSTED AND READ THE CAPTIONS. That says it all. I hate giving bad reviews but honestly, if I could give this no stars I would. PLEASE DON'T WASTE YOUR MONEY!
I have been using ARAMIS for a very long time and I still think is has the best scent for an average American man. I have used others from time to time but always go back to ARAMIS

Listing is very misleading.  I only received a single twin pack of this deodorant.  Extremely overpriced if the listing is only for a single twin pack and not a "Twin Pack, Pack of Two" as listed in the title.
Arrived on time. Just enough color/tint so lips aren't boring. Hydrates lips nicely. Recommend.

A regular disposable razor has a more slim profile for travel purposes.  I'd skip this purported "travel" size and go with a regular disposable razor or a better version of this brand razor in full size.
I have been using this aftershave for years and I never had any bad comments about the odor. On the contrary, I get nothing but compliments. Especially from the ladies

I NEED YOUR OPINION!

I Just got this lotion. I already tried the Paint it Black recently and liked it so I thought I would try Black Storm. This lotion smells a lot like the Hawaiian Tropic tanning oil you get from Wal-Mart or Walgreens. It kind of threw me off and made me wonder if it's not authentic and mixed with other things? I have been tanning for years and have never smelled that odor on an indoor tanning lotion.

Do you all have the same odor to the lotion? Maybe that's just the way it smells and I'm not used to it. I haven't tried it in a tanning bed yet because for some reason the smell and the way it felt on my skin made me think twice about it. I think I may return it just want another person's opinion.

Thanks!

Scent is nothing. Not there. Put in a shoe, my walker bag, on my favorite Covid mask. There's a vague scent for a second or two then. . . nothing whatsoever. These are so small they're hard to handle. I had to photograph the back of the box (about the size of two pennies) and blow up the image to read the instructions. I don't know what these are really designed for but I can't smell anything moments after opening the package (no, it's not a Covid side effect) so I can't see any reason to have these around unless you'd like poorly shaped four-leaf clovers to stick to things. Not worth anything.
Works really well exactly as described but it's lacking that smell that most Paul Mitchell products have this one just doesn't smell very good

I have used Mitchum off and on for decades.  This gel, however fails me every time I try it.  The solids and soft solids work fine.  The solids last much longer than the soft solids.  Haven't used a roll on in quite a few years.  The solids are where I put my money.
Was supposed to hv a free surprise inside and it did not, my niece was crushed!

I don't have a problem with some woman's cologne. There's quite a few with tones and scents that are unisex. This is a bit feminine but a decent scent overall. Right on the box though it says it's to 'Attract Male'. That being said I'm not looking to attract anyone, I just like to smell good on the rare occasions I go out. Wish they'd written the product description better I'd probably have skipped this one but they said it was a scent for men not to attract men.

The first problem I had with this is the handle is kinda short and makes it hard to reach all of my back. Good for doing my legs but the handle needs to be a little longer and needs a bend to make washing your back easier.
Second problem is you're not supposed to use this on your delicate parts or your face. This means you have to put a large amount of soap on this to do the majority of your body and then more soap on your pouf or washcloth to wash the rest of you. It's wasteful.
My third issue is charging. I don't have a big counter in my bathroom and only have one outlet so I need to find a place to stash the proprietary (ugh!) charging cable and then take it out of the bathroom once it's dry to charge it. Too much work. I can get just as clean with a pouf.

I have very thick and straight hair and this didn't do anything - not even add volume. It has a tiny bit of a curl for half an hour, then goes away. better get a product with smaller diameter rolls then brush it out for real curls.
returned
Exactly as advertised!
Exactly as advertised!

I havwe been using this product for years and I think it suits me perfectly. I never met anyone who didn't like the order. My grandaughter loveed this so much when I wore it that she recently visited and Took a couple of my hats I had and sprayed them with Aramis to remind her of me .How great is that!!!!!
Exploded on me, would never buy again!

My mom, whom I live with and care for, has, at age 87, developed thinning hair and searches for volumizing  products to help her look as nice as possible. This shampoo left her hair stringy and lifeless with a greasy look the same day she washed her hair. Getting a pleasing lather took quite a bit of product and effort. It did rinse easily but did nothing for her hair. We've had store brand products give better results.
Exactly as advertised!
Heated up nicely, but it is really too small for hair that is medium in length.

These stickers are nice. That's about as far as I'm willing to go on their praise. They're nice. The biggest issue is the stickers have a border of additional plastic. I get that it has to be there otherwise they would shred trying to get them off the backing paper. The problem is the plastic they're printed on is cheap. It isn't very clear so even on a light grey background you can see the border standing out like a sore thumb. It's too visible. It detracts from the look. Also, because of their size, the glow in the dark material lasts a couple minutes at best. It just doesn't glow long enough to be cool.

This a decent man's cologne with a reasonable longevity to the scent. There a very intense citrus note for the first ten to fifteen minutes but that lifts a bit and then the scent lasts a few hours. I enjoyed this. The bottle is striking and makes a very nice gift.

Edited review: THIS ITEM STOPPED WORKING AFTER 65 DAYS - VERY DISAPPOINTING SINCE IT WAS ONE OF THE BEST ITEMS IN TERMS OF WORKING ON AN ISSUE  BUT SINCE IT STOPPED WORKING. IT IS NO LONGER WORTH THE MONEY- if you have dry cracked feet this is a product for you. I have tried foot peels, scrubs & regular pumice stones - nothing worked like this product. My feet are finally smooth! The product is easy to use & clean. Will be buying these for gifts for my friends for years to come. Great product.

Best part here are the gloves, rough enough to actually exfoliate, easy to use. The main body brush is too soft for what I wanted. The only saving grace is the long handle that will get those out of reach areas. The facial brush is unneeded with the gloves.
The only cold cream I use. It's strong smelling, but has the lovely tingle and keeps my skin clean and soft.
I ordered this for the price and the good reviews but the color “pink” looks much different online than in person. It is VERY pink. Highly pigmented and cool toned. Not for me.
Great product, not available locally so this was the only way I could try this. Look forward to trying other Frizz Ease product with the help of Amazon.

Probably one of the worst shampoos ever. The DAY it's used the hair is limp, lifeless and looking stringy and greasy. Care was made to completely rinse the product out but after multiple uses this product does a horrible job on the hair. The scent wasn't all that great either. Very unimpressed with this product.
I was very disappointed. I didn't get the results that all the 5 star reviews did. Not even near those results

This thing does not remove facial hair accept in spots, not worth 29 cents let alone 29 doallars.  I got mine a lot cheaper thank goodness.  But I recomment you pass this thing by.  save your money its just not worth it.
Great purple love it

This makeup is a true full coverage foundation unlike others that make the claim.  Because I have dry skin I find that I really have to prep well with moisturizer and primer, otherwise the makeup fails to adhere properly and looks terrible.  With a good prep, this makeup looks beautiful. You need to work fast with a sponge and it helps to do your face in  sections.  Seems to work with both silicon and non-silicon primers.
Great for hands, not so great on feet.

First and foremost after their maximum seven (7) minutes the test patch I tried on the side of my head had the same hair it had seven minutes prior. This did nothing. Well, not nothing, it was a mild irritant and stunk to high heaven. I've smelled something chemically similar in the past but I can't recall what. It smells revolting. Whomever coined the 'light fresh scent' statement on the tube had one evil sense of humor.
I'm not sure that had it worked I'd have wanted to use the product. I tested a bit bigger area on the side of my head and even after washing it off three or four times and using a spritz of cologne I could still smell it! I can't imagine the stench had I covered my scalp with this stuff. Shaving with my electric razor is much preferable.
The irritation was a mild burning sensation and a bit of redness that lasted a few hours. Nothing dramatic but just another reason NOT to use this.
Not recommended.

My mom used about half to three quarters of the bottle following the instructions. While it did seem to produce a stronger nail she also complained about a drastic increase in deep hang nails down into the quick. Painful, hard to resolve, often bloody messes that took time to heal. She stopped using the product and while she still has the occasional issue it's not like when she used this every day. Didn't do enough good to outweigh the discomfort.
Great neutral color but wears off quickly lips feel moist must keep reapplying other than that it goes without saying that it’s a great brand as always
Works ok need practice
Leaked after awhile so became useless...
Sorry this item made my eyes itchy. That made me rub them so much my eye got a ruptured vessel. So it all rubbed off immediately. Waste of money for my sensitive skin

This oil works great to help smooth down my hair after I flat iron it.  I also use it on the bottom few inches of my hair to reduce knots.  It works and has no fragrance or chemical additives.  I only need one pump to cover all of my hair (shoulder length and very thick), so the bottle will last a long time.  I will be keeping this product on my hair care must have list.  This product does not add any product buildup to my hair, so I can straighten my hair a second day before washing it again.
These clips hold your hair in place without falling down. They are very comfortable and does not damage your hair. I would recommend them as an item to try.

I decided to try this product because it was rated much lower on the Think Dirty app than the primer I was using.  It goes on smoothly and easily and leaves a silky smooth type of feeling after application.  I did not need to allow any time for drying before adding my foundation.  It seems to work just as well as my previous primer, but it s much cleaner (rated 3 on Think Dirty app).  I will be buying this again, unless I find a 0 rated primer first.
Stays put under your mask! Love it. Dry but color last for hours.

My first impression on opening the package was "This can't be a Braun." my second was "Wow, that's cheap!" Lightweight & very thin plastic. I'm disabled with nerve damage that has caused serious issues with my hands; I found the adjustment dial to be quite hard to turn due to it being so low and nearly flush with the body of the trimmer. The comb has trouble staying on the clippers when you change lengths rapidly or move from short to long and back. It binds and comes part or all the way off. It's easy enough to put back on but still annoying. There's no visual charge level and only a single light on the charging plug to tell it's operating. The power switch is also hard to operate but does lock positively on and off. As clippers, with the comb on, they work okay. I trimmed my goatee to a good length with the longest setting. I'd like longer but that's rarely offered. My biggest problem arises when the comb is removed. I like the slightly scruffy, clippered look and hate razor burn on my cheeks so I've used clippers in lieu of shaving for decades the back of the blades which is supposed to glide across your skin is made of ribbed plastic which caught and stuck on my face and was very actually painful to use. I can't believe a company like Braun would put out a product as cheap and badly designed as this.

I wanted to love this stuff so bad but I just can't. It's awful. Goes on so slippery and I actually think that my makeup goes on worse with it on than without it. My foundation completely wipes away with even the slightest touch and it lessens the coverage somehow? Also, my usually long-wearing foundation is gone after an hour or two? It does the opposite of what it's supposed to do. Never buying it again. Probably throwing it away.

Product arrived open and leaking.
Sticky mess
I didn’t read the description fully. I wanted a traditional scarf. Having said that, this scarf is well made and I am keeping them.
Love the smell of this body wash. Leaves my skin feeling soft and smooth.
Great for those with sensitive skin, and older folks whose skin is more delicate. stops the bleeding quickly and finally

Awful... sprays very heavily and it's rubs off on anything that touches your hair. Even your fingers. Doesn't ever fully dry and if you scratch your scalp, your fingers will be covered in the spray. I woke up with splotches all over my pillow from this stuff. Never buying again.
When you ask for a refund and Amazon decides to replace the. Item it’s a bad look.
Love love love it but it’s really small

I purchased this as an experiment to see if I could reasonably do my own gel nails at home. The price was less than one visit to the salon - so why not. The verdict - great choice! I really like this nail lamp. Just to be clear, this is an UV lamp. It takes 120 seconds (2 minutes) to cure the nail polish. When I researched UV versus LED lamps, I decided to go with UV because it would cure any nail polish, not just special LED curable ones.

The lamp has a setting that will leave it on until you turn it off and a setting for timer that can be used with the 120 seconds switch in the back. This allows you to just hit the switch and it will turn off when your 2 minutes are up. The opening/tray on this is large enough to put my full hand in (including thumbs) and large enough for my husbands significantly larger hands. I can also fit my toes in the opening. The sliding tray is pretty helpful for not bumping your fingers on the top when removing your hands.

I used gel polish I got through a groupon deal to try this out and it worked very well. I'm still getting the hang of polishing my nails (please don't judge my pics), but they are nice and hard and set to a hard and beautiful shine. Make sure to follow whatever directions your polish has (including using rubbing alcohol to set the polish). All in all, for less than a manicure costs, I think this baby packs a mean punch!
Love the results. Best permanent hair coloring product I have tried!

The cutting heads are even unlike some other foil razors which had stepped blades. The shaver is a bit narrow so large areas like legs, heads, etc. The biggest issue with this razor like a couple others I've tried the power button is placed in the middle of the side of the razor. So, as you're changing your grip to shave different areas it's really easy to accidentally hit the power button as you're shaving. It's just an annoyance but it is annoying.

this smells amazing..Its Musk its not meant to be a strong scent.. I have bought so many diffrent Musks oils and this one is one of my favorites. Naked Musk from Amica is my Favorite but this is right up there at the top of my list of favorites..I recommend this one if you want that Coty Wild Musk similar smell. Love it.
There are no specific directions on how to use this product. There are some directions on the back of the box, but beginners would have no clue on what it means.
Awesome mascara. Made lashes where there were none! My new favorite 🤩 smudges but still haven’t seen lashes like this in years! Lol 😂

Still learning how to use it,but it is very sturdy and works great.Much better price than some I have seen.Would give it 5 stars but I don't shave
every day only 2-3 times a week.

I really disliked this shampoo. Usually I like a product with a strong scent but this product's scent brings back memories of old hair salons and my god mother's cellar where she cut hair (mine badly) for decades. The product lathers and rinses well and while it got my hair clean it left it flat and lifeless the several times I used it. This isn't a product I'd recommend or use again.

I use this primarily for my face. I put this on in the morning and it stays all day.

But make sure to wash it off at night though and clean your face properly. The items is very good but I imagine it does stick to the pores. I did get a few pimples if I left them overnight (too tired to wash face) but after I started to wash my face with amild face wash before bed, didn't see those anymore.

I love the Neutrogena smell.

Usage is just like any other sunblock. Even though I apply it by gliding the applicator (sort of like a deodorant stick) over my face, I still have to use my finger tips to spread it around. Expecting it to just sort of dissipate all over your face or other parts of the body without rubbing to spread it doesn't make sense to me.

But with nano-technology around, maybe a product like that exists?

I'm a Realtree girl! I found this item by accident so I bought it. I like the scents & will wear them but they are a bit too girly for me. I prefer earthier smells. The package was slightly damaged but all three bottles were undamaged.

I bought this in an attempt to recreate the color I got from my favorite Rimmel purple lipstick (which I can't seem to find anymore). If you're looking for matter coverage and a color that will pop, save your money. This isn't it. This lipstick is thin. And I mean, super thin. No matter how much of it I put on, I could still see my lips through. And it's not matte. It doesn't seem to dry, either. It wipes off on everything. The color of the lipstick is definitely not the color you'll see on your lips. Huge disappointment.
Smells like men’s deodorant and it lingers throughout the entire day and didn’t work very well.
Good product,
Exactly as advertised!

I like most colognes. I'm always open to new and different scents. . . until I tried this. Expecting something nice I was foolishly liberal with my first application. I was in the shower five minutes later. The first scent that came to mind was celery which, while not a terrible smell, is not a scent I want to wear. I sat for a few minutes to give it a chance and then I picked up the distinct odor of rancid watermelon (I unfortunately know all too well what that smells like). It wasn't strong but it was there. I asked my mother whst she thought. She both checked out how I smelled and tried it on herself. While she didn't rush to wash it off like I did she said she didn't care for it and wouldn't wear it. My neighbor was over for a visit, it gave her a headache and she disliked the scent. Definitely something I hope I never smell again.
Did not like the product
Love the soft feel

THE REMOTE CORD SHOULD BE 6 FEET LONGER.  THERE IS NO WAY TO KEEP THE MODE ON ONE NUMBER.  IT CYCLES THROUGH ALL 5 AND I DON'T LIKE THEM ALL.  I WISH I COULD SET IT TO THE ONE I LIKE AND LEAVE IT THERE.  IF THERE IS A WAY I COULDN'T TELL I DIDN'T RECEIVE A USER MANUAL.  I GUESS THEY FIGURE WE DON'T NEED ONE SINCE IT DOESN'T DO ANYTHING EXCEPT ANNOY THE HE** OUT OF ME!  I CONTACTED SELLER TO FIND OUT WHAT M1-M5 MEANT.  ABSOLUTELY USELESS ANSWER.  I WILL NOT BUY FROM THIS COMPANY AGAIN EVEN IF I HAVE TO BUY FROM ANOTHER SITE
Not a tea rose at all more like flat mauve.
As always I order what I think is the right color and when it comes its so much darker.....I don't know why I order color online because it never works out.......

The color was exactly as pictured.  Application was a little difficult because the wand is angled on one side and made it awkward to apply to my left eye using my right hand.  The reason I do not like this product is because the eye shadow color faded throughout the day and was almost completely gone by the end of my work day (approximately nine hours after application).  If you wanted to maintain the color, you would have to apply the eye shadow multiple times per day.  I will be throwing out this eye shadow and continuing to search for another option.
It looks like a single use disposable set. And I haven’t found it to work well either. I would not recommend this at all. Pretty much a waste.

I really like Hipsy headbands - I own several of the solid colors. I think the warning on this one is that even though it's patchwork, it's still kind of that plastic material, so it just ends up looking kind of weird. Also the plastic is thick enough that when you look at the headband on your head from the front you see the side and it's just white (like with the foamy filling stuff).

Thought I'd try this as an alternative to my other long lasting lipstick: what a joke this brand is!!

After it dried, it stayed on for less than 3 hours!! Absolutely no "vinyl" appearance. My lips were sticky regardless of whether I applied 1 coat or several. Yes, I shook the bottle.

As far as the Captivated color, it is a TAWNY pink.

Save your money and buy another brand.
Nice but won’t last reapplied often
Exactly as advertised!

Nice little tool but cheaply made. One of the attachments came apart when I started to use it, but was able to put back together.

For the price, it will work

They work well and give good service, but they are very uncomfortable in the hand because of their design, and often slip sideways when you squeeze the trigger, requiring you to hold onto the bottle as well.  Good value, though.
It didn't work when I got it

These are nice, thick and black but they have a little too much criss-cross to them. They don't look like real, natural lashes on their own. They are not too long so if you wear thick eyeliner and shadow, you may be able to pass them off as natural
This did not work for me at all. It turns blue when you apply it to the lash and there’s no adhesion. Eyelashes kept coming up. Never buying again.
Great for yourself face

Love the way this softens my dry lips and the colors are soft. Just smells and tastes like petroleum . They need to add better flavoring and fragrance. I love the push button tubes and the look of the flowers.
Color chart is way off way lighter product than pictures can’t use this item. Waste of money
Too big to look natural

I’ve been doing my own nails for 10+ years so I’ve experienced my share of good and bad top coats. This wins the prize for absolute WORST. So bad it’s going in trash. It doesn’t dry. Like ever. I’m 12 hours post application and it still feels sticky and gummy. Ruined my manicure and I have to redo both feet and hands. Buy glisten and glow top coat instead.
Classic pink peachy rose.

All in all, I really like this Matte Lip Color. It's not insanely bright and goes on very smoothly. It's not a lip stain, so it does fade a bit with wear. I think how much it fades will depend on the normal stuff (how much you drink, eat, rub your lips, etc.) and if you top it with a gloss. Overall, at the end of a normal day, I can still see the color on my lips.

As for the color, the picture doesn't offer a swipe, but the bottom of the container makes it look pretty dark wine. It's a nice wine color, but I found it to be a bit lighter than I expected. It certainly isn't rocket red or anything, but lighter than expected. I posted pictures of the color on my lips for reference.

I have lots of holographic nail polish. This one is very disappointing.  I expected to be able to turn my thumb, up, down, back and forth and see a rainbow of colors.  Copper metallic, that’s it. No hints of red, orange, gold or yellow and whatever else is in the description.

The scent is nice, I really do like it. But this goes on oily as it's an oil base, so I can't just spray my scalp or armpits with it. You need to apply small drops with the applicator which means you overly oil your skin or the scent goes away because you didn't use enough. I can't say anything about the attractant of the pheromones, no women tore my clothes off or even noticed the scent when I was around them.
Good scent, kinda oily, doesn't draw in the women (never expected it to).

It get's the job done as in it removes the make-up, if you want to use like 5 of these little things per eye. The circles are pretty small and pretty thin, so I had to use multiple to get a smokey eye off. It's not greasy or oily, but it can kind of burn if you open your eyes or the liquid seeps in. It's not like an awful burn, but a bit startling. I have found that the best use for these is to clean off any eyeshadow dust from application that gets on my cheek or beside my eyes, it's pretty good for precision removal.

What a piece of crap!  I read the instructions and inserted fresh batteries.  That's the last thing that worked right.  Tried cutting some of my beard, and the blades grabbed the hair and hung on, stopping completely.  I tried different batteries with the same results.  I oiled the blades, same result.  Following the instructions, I removed the bottom blade, only to have the spring loaded assembly shoot half way across the room and dismantle itself.  Tried the single "trimmer" blade, with the same "can't cut crap" results.  I threw it in the trash.  Also, no cleaning brush.
Have had no complaint it’s moisture and no reaction to sensitive eye area.
Exactly as advertised!
Cannot & will not recommend.

Upon reading the description, I was concerned that perhaps the lipstick would be too oily. Actually, it was TOO DRY. It’s ridiculously overpriced. The color is very natural, but I was expecting it to actually moisturize. I will stay with Neutrogena MoistureSmooth Color Stick. It has much more moisture and it’s less than 1/4 the price.

This thing saved my life during my last weave. I hate itchy scalp and certain weave patterns make it very hard to reach your scalp, this tool is perfect. I used it to massage my scalp when washing and just on a regular basis. I actually ordered two so I could keep on at home and one in my purse. It's best a week or so after install when your hair is a bit looser and it just slides right in there. Perfect and much more effective than the head pat.
I really like it. Works great. Doesn't seem that rugged, though.
Just started using this product easy to use, apply very little product.

I have been searching for a matte powder to use on top of foundation that was cleaner than the Clinique brand loose powder I have been using for years.  This product rated zero on Think Dirty app, so I figured it was worth a try.  The lightest shade (0-1) was a little too light for me if I have been in the sun at all, but it is good enough to use in winter (I wear Linen in BeautyCounter Tint Skin Hydrating Foundation).  I will go up a shade for any future order.  The powder is difficult to dispense (there are only four tiny holes in the container), but it goes on easily and adds a matte finish to my foundation.  I have to apply the powder about halfway through the day (oily skin in a humid area), but I consider reapplication worth it to use a cleaner product.
Like this mascara

I have natural hair and used this as I transitioned. I first want to say, this stuff smells AWESOME - like pudding or something. It was pretty awesome. It also worked very well, My hair felt beautiful afterwards.

Company was great on credit however beware of major exfoliating adjunct to regular skin care not a peek off mask. There only so many hours in the day and item desc6 matters, I will say pleasant smelling when I did test on my hand and did appear to exfoliate but not significantly.

Love it and find I cannot buy this shampoo  in the stores.  All come with conditioner but don't want that. Last one I bought on Amazon was 25.3 ounces for $5.99 and this turned out to be only 12 ounces for $9.99. I didn't look at the size when ordering, but assumed it was the same I purchased before, so unfortunately I will not be buying again at this increased price. Shame on the company ):
Scent nice, but weak
Tossed it out. Sticky and old. Do not recommend this seller
its ok
Maybe I expected too much. I hoped for something to really SCRUB my skin. These don't do it at all. I can do a better job with my bare hands and a good oatmeal soap.
Great

The size is good but the material looks really cheap. It is a thick plastic that has a smell that I’m sure will resolve in time. I had a choice of patterns but chose the solid with black slash marks—-it looks like dollar store quality. The price was good and I think I’ll fill them with toiletries snd donate to the shelter.

First I want to say though I haven't tried all of the varieties that came in this box the several I have used smelled great!
But I have run into several issues:
• First this is something you need to unwrap as you're getting into the shower. These are tightly wrapped and the plastic is, while protective, a pain to completely remove. Twice now I've ended up with bits of plastic littering the shower stall.
• Building on the first issue these pucks are quite soft. I've used other shower pucks from brands like Vicks and they're many times harder and to be honest, easier to cut out of their wrappings.
• I guess building on the first two problems because they're so soft these are nearly completely dissolved before I'm even done shaving my head (something I do with a blade razor while sitting in the shower) so half my shower smells good or I have to come up with a way to have a second soft, crumbly, shower puck on hand but dry and within reach so once the first is gone I can start on a second.
So I guess this can be boiled down to two annoying issues:
1 • They're a pain to unwrap. The plastic used is terrible.
2 • They're not pressed hard enough so they dissolve way too quick.
I really like the several scents I've tried and look forward to trying the rest over time. While I'd like to see the pucks pressed a bit harder I wouldn't want them as compressed as the Vicks, those barely are gone at the end of the shower. Somewhere in the middle would be nice.
Great smell but a little pricey!

howdy y'all,

the actual item does it's job well. i would rate it a 3 on it's own. it feels good in the hand, cuts easily and cleans easily, too. i don't trim very short, so that hasn't been a problem for me like it has been for others.

the reason i rated this item at ONE is cuz the blister pack had NO indication of how to open it. it was sealed TIGHTLY and the plastic was quite brittle. i finally took a knife and the package out in the yard and sliced it open. the plastic shattered when the cut neared one edge and i was struck by many bits of sharp plastic.

i can't recommend anyone buying this item while the blister pack remains so remarkably dangerous to open.

i presume they hoped any shoplifters would be covered in blood ... but they forgot to account for actual buyers. [*sigh ...*]

take care,
lee

ps- this is the 2nd panasonic item i've bought with nearly customer-proof, dang dangerous packaging. they need to rethink their packaging.
-lee

I put Epsom salts in the medicine box/compartment & they were dissolved & disbursed into the water quickly & completely. I started with warm water & only used it for 20 minutes so I can't speak to it heating or maintaining water temperature until I use it longer. I turned on the red light but turned it off within about 5 minutes because I didn't see that it did anything for me. My only issue with this product is that it's a bit noisier than I expected & that's disappointing but not a deal breaker. Overall, I really like this product & the price is right so I definitely recommend it.

These multihead razors work best when held in reverse, the blades in your palm and the body on the backs of your fingers. This razor does not fit well in that position at all. Worse the three heads conform very poorly to the scalp. Shaving my head was painful, rough and it did not provide a very close shave. Even worse than that when pressing down to try and get a close shave even in high power mode (this has two power modes) you can hear the motor struggling to keep up. As a razor it's not great. It works okay as a face scrubber with the white brush. I found using it as a set of clippers to be very awkward. The round body is strange to hold when trying to do precise trimming. The one adapter I found to be very useful? The nose and ear hair trimmer. The ball shaped motor housing it quite easy to hold when stuffing the cutter into your nostril or running it around the edge of your ear hole. With the high power mode mode it does this job nicely and the battery life when trimming just your nose and occasionally the stray ear hair. Otherwise this unit is fairly useless. I'm going to keep it as a nose/ear hair trimmer.

(This is the review of a performer, who over decades has worked with numerous different types and brands of products.) I bought this product because I have bought other products from this company that I liked, and was looking for a matte finish gloss that wouldn't cost $15+ dollars. This seemed ideal. First, I put this product on dry, unprimered, unchapped lips. Immediately, it smelled awful. The color does not go on evenly, and settles in to every line and pore. It dried quickly, and felt sticky. I figured as it dried more, that stickiness would disappear (the way many glosses do). It doesn't. It stays sticky. The worst part is that after five minutes, when I pressed my lips together, the color "broke", so I had to reapply, which made the color even more uneven. I decided to start over. I tried to remove it with a makeup remover cloth. That was an awful experience. It was painful, and the color left a stain on my skin. I put on Chapsick first to make my skin less dry and a bit more soft. After the Chapstick dried, I reapplied the color. The color was lighter, went on smoother, but was still uneven. It settles into the creases and corners, and requires blotting with the fingers, which then stains your fingertips. It was passable, but still sticky, but the color eventually darkened to that black currant color on the chart (I have olive/tan skin). It's not worth it, though, when there are less expensive options which don't end up sticky and don't cause this much trouble. Also, this lipstick comes off on everything your lips touch as you wear it, including but not limited to - cups, straws, silverware, and children.

I have been tanning for 7 years (on and off, that is) and have never been so satisfied with a tanning lotion. I really feel darker than I have ever been. Now, granted, it may be because I have been laying in a realllly good bed, but still the lotion has helped I think. Not to mention, it smells and makes your skin feel amazing.

DOWNSIDE:

When I received the product it had some of the lotion (which is a tan color) dried around the top of the bottle opening. I don't know if this is just a really old bottle, or someone had tampered with it or used it? But there was definitely some dried residue around the bottle and the inside of the cap. And it did NOT look like that had just happened through shipping, it looked like it had been there for a long time. Makes me wonder.....

Overall I recommend it, its definitely cheaper on here than stuff you get at a tanning salon that probably wont work as well.

Ouch! I have a small trimmer with a similar sized head so I was excited to try out this model which has a longer reach. I don't shave, I go for the close scruff look done with no attachments on the trimmer. The first thing I noticed was this hurt, quite a lot. It didn't get better as I did both cheeks and around my goatee. I couldn't believe how painful this was to use. No other trimmer and I've used many over the decades, has ever been this rough on my face. The three combs that come with the trimmer are too close in size to be very useful or versatile. The foil head that comes with this kit does a mediocre job. After going over my face with the trimmer the foil head seemed to take off next to nothing and left my face looking like a clipper cut.

So this is about as light as it appears and that probably doesn't matter a whole lot for a primer. The better question is how it interacts with the eyeshadow. I've used MAC, Clinique, and Urban Decay eyelid primers. I love my urban decay eyelid primer - it's the perfect shade for me.

I found this relatively easy to put on and I was able to use a sponge to rub it in so the color almost didn't matter, it just added a slight lightening of the eye. In terms of primer, this gets the job done. I think the Urban Decay is better at keeping my eyeshadow in place and allowing me to blend the way I want to, but for the price, this works. I added a picture of the raw product and it rubbed in a bit on my arm for reference.

One last note, like other reviewers, I thought the bottle felt a bit empty. This didn't last very long - again for the price that could be expected, but you'll be purchasing this frequently if you do your make-up daily.

I bought the RoC Retinol Correxion, Deep Wrinkle Serum a month ago after reading the good reviews it received from other consumers.  Not knowing the age, or skin type of the other fine reviewers I was just hopeful that it would work for me...sadly it hasn't seemed to make any difference in my wrinkles whatsoever.  I will tell you that I am 44 years old.  I am brunette and didn't really get any wrinkles until I hit my 40's.  I have an olive complexion and try not to spend too much time in the sun (I do live in Arizona however) so when I do go out into the sun of course I use sun screen. I only have wrinkles on my forehead but there are two deep wrinkles right where my eyebrows meet my nose (on each side, I don't have a uni-brow just yet:) that make me look like I'm always scowling.  Anyhoo, I have been applying the serum to my forehead every day, twice a day for about a month now and nothing has happened.  Nada, zip, zilch, zero, difference.  I still have my DEEP wrinkles on my forehead and the two DEEP wrinkles above the bridge of my nose.  In fact I would say that the added moisture to my skin has kind of made it more oily thus causing breakouts...YUCK.  So I continue to look like an angry brunette (I look angry because of those nasty wrinkles between my eyebrows) and I'm out $20 bucks (including shipping).  I would say if you have dry skin this couldn't hurt because at least it will moisturize but if you have naturally oily skin you might want to reconsider...unless of course your partial to adult acne.

Edit: I had reviewed this just the other day. This review hasn't gone live yet. I've used this razor five times to shave my head prior to today. The sixth time, today, I shaved my head with a clean razor. I always clean up when I use it. Today was no exception. I shaved then went to open the shave heads to rinse them out and the very first one snapped, the cover on the bottom fell away. The razor head completely detached from the unit. There are several broken shards of plastic where the hinge that allows the heads to open shattered. Razor is now useless and in the trash. At least I got six shaves before it died. Rating dropped from 4-stars to 1-star. I didn't use excessive force. I own several razors in this style, though this was the first 7-head unit. My 5-head is over a year old, been used a dozen or more times and is fine. Very disappointed in this product.

I have several multiple head razors 5,6 and now this 7 head model. These give a good, reasonably close shave. The only problem I've had with all of these units is I, as you can see in the picture, wear a goatee and every single one of these razors will grab beard hair and tear it the hell out. Besides that though when you use these with a positive grip they're easy to use and give a good shave. Battery life is pretty impressive. I shave my head, neck and cheeks and get easily seven to ten shaves before it's down low enough I feel the need to recharge. Another bonus is it seems all these heads are interchangeable so if one's low on charge or fits your hand better you can just swap to a different handle or head. However you like.

I usually use L'Oreal products and I am used to using a much lighter colour when it states that the pigment is for fair complexion.  I am also accustomed to a much lighter feel of a B.B. product, but I suppose that is because it doesn't have any sunscreen in it.  I read several positive  reviews on this B.B. cream, so I decided to try it.  I apply my B.B. cream mixed with my moisturizer so it isn't so "thick'.  When I mixed this with my moisturizer it still maintained it's thickness.  I imagine that the high S.P.F. of 45 had a lot to do with it. This has more pigment than I care for.  My other B.B. cream is white and turns to beige upon application, but when I wipe in on my towel , it. doesn't leave a beige mark. I notice that this has a lot more beige pigment in it especially when I wipe it on my towel almost reminding me of makeup but much more dilute, however, still a little bit bothersome to me.  I have ruddy skin and what I was looking for in a B.B. cream is something to hide the redness when I blush. which is frequently. This is kind of beigish and I haven''t seen myself blush in the mirror yet. I do know that I will use up this tube over the summer on the occasions that I will got to public places where I may become redder., but I don't think I will buy it again.
